title: Thelypteris chandrae
taxonomy: Thelypteris chandrae is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as the marsh fern family. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics, which are closely related to other species within the family. Thelypteris chandrae is a genus of ferns, and it belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The plant's classification is further narrowed down to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification system helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and patterns of diversity within the fern community.

intro: Thelypteris chandrae, also known as the Chandra's maiden fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in India, due to its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris chandrae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris chandrae is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
